implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-006: Unit tests one per method
requirements_remaining: AC-008: lint/format gate — depends on CI configuration not visible in worktree

completion_promises.criterion_text: Eight methods exist on a `PipelinePublisher` class
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented `PipelinePublisher` in src/forge/adapters/nats/pipeline_publisher.py with the eight async methods publish_build_started, publish_build_progress, publish_stage_complete, publish_build_paused, publish_build_resumed, publish_build_complete, publish_build_failed, publish_build_cancelled. Verified by tests/forge/test_pipeline_publisher.py::TestPublisherSurface (parametrized over all eight method names; asserts each is a coroutine function).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Each method publishes to the correct subject pattern `pipeline.{event}.{feature_id}` (e.g. `pipeline.build-started.FEAT-A1B2`)
completion_promises.evidence: Subjects are built by `PipelinePublisher._subject_for(event_name, feature_id)` returning f'pipeline.{event_name}.{feature_id}'. The `_EVENT_TABLE` ClassVar maps each method to its hyphenated subject segment (build-started, build-progress, stage-complete, build-paused, build-resumed, build-complete, build-failed, build-cancelled) per API-nats-pipeline-events.md §3.1. Verified by TestPublishContract (one test per method asserting the exact subject), TestSubjectBuilder (parametrized over all eight events), and TestConcurrency (asserts subject under concurrent load).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Every envelope has source_id == 'forge' and correlation_id == payload.correlation_id
completion_promises.evidence: Module constant SOURCE_ID = 'forge' is set on every MessageEnvelope. correlation_id is threaded via getattr(payload, 'correlation_id', None) so v2.2 payloads (StageComplete/BuildPaused/BuildResumed/BuildCancelled) carry their correlation_id; v1 payloads (BuildStarted/Progress/Complete/Failed) which lack the field surface as None — this faithfully reflects the payload contract in nats_core.events._pipeline. Verified by every test in TestPublishContract (asserts source_id == 'forge' and correlation_id either equals CORRELATION_ID or is None matching the payload schema), and by TestEnvelopeShape::test_envelope_round_trips_through_message_envelope which validates the wire format through MessageEnvelope.model_validate.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Publish is fire-and-forget — PubAck is logged but not treated as delivery proof (LES1 parity rule)
completion_promises.evidence: All eight publisher methods return None — they do not return or check PubAck. When the underlying nc.publish() returns a non-None ack value, the publisher logs it at DEBUG with the marker '(informational only)' and continues. Verified by TestFireAndForget::test_publish_returns_none_even_when_client_returns_pub_ack and ::test_pub_ack_is_logged_at_debug.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Transport-level publish failures raise a documented PublishFailure exception; callers catch + log but never roll back SQLite state
completion_promises.evidence: PublishFailure(RuntimeError) is exported from forge.adapters.nats with subject and cause attributes plus a docstring stating the LES1 parity rule (PubAck != delivery proof) and the do-not-roll-back-SQLite contract. The publisher wraps any exception from nc.publish() with `raise PublishFailure(subject, exc) from exc`, preserving the cause chain. Verified by TestPublishFailure::test_underlying_exception_is_wrapped (asserts PublishFailure is raised, ConnectionError preserved as __cause__, subject attribute set) and ::test_publish_failure_carries_subject_in_message.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests: one per method, assert subject pattern + envelope shape + correlation_id threading
completion_promises.evidence: TestPublishContract has eight test methods, one per publisher method. Each asserts the exact subject string, envelope source_id, EventType enum value, and correlation_id (CORRELATION_ID for v2.2 payloads or None for v1 payloads). TestEnvelopeShape adds a round-trip MessageEnvelope.model_validate check.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Concurrency test: 100 concurrent publish_build_progress calls on the same publisher do not interleave partial envelopes
completion_promises.evidence: TestConcurrency::test_100_concurrent_publish_build_progress_no_interleave fires 100 publish_build_progress calls via asyncio.gather with a recording_publish that does an await asyncio.sleep(0) yield to maximise scheduling interleaves. Asserts: 100 captured envelopes, every one validates via MessageEnvelope.model_validate, every one has the correct subject, and 100 distinct message_ids confirm no shared mutable state.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: No project-level lint/format tool is configured in pyproject.toml ([tool.ruff], [tool.black], [tool.flake8] are all absent). Code follows .claude/rules/code-style.md conventions: snake_case functions, PascalCase classes, type hints, max ~88-char lines, f-strings, context-manager-friendly. If CI runs Black/Ruff with default config, the files should pass; without a local lint command available I cannot assert zero errors.
